summary: An average‑quality audience tape of a lively 1981 show, highlighted by strong performances of Cassidy, Althea, Let It Grow and To Lay Me Down.
review: The concert captures an energetic first set and a more relaxed second set, with several fans singling out songs like Cassidy and To Lay Me Down as standout moments. Recording quality is described as average to fair – decent equipment but hampered by the hall’s acoustics – making the listening experience mixed.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (average ~4/5) while noting only average to fair recording quality; combined weighted score yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Equipment described as three Nakamichi mics into a D5, with no hi‑hat issues; however reviewers note the hall sounded like a barn and overall sound is average compared to other era tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set pulsing with energy, second set a bit more human but overall solid; some sloppy moments in the first set are mentioned.
